# Platform Readiness Checklist: Multi-Platform GameBase Libraries

**Purpose**: Validate that requirements for Atari 800 platform support, platform workflow parity, import, settings, and launch behavior are complete, clear, consistent, and ready for implementation planning.
**Created**: 2026-06-27
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are platform selection requirements complete for first-run, returning-user, imported-platform, and unimported-platform startup states? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK002 Are Atari 800-specific requirements documented for MDB import, required folders, RetroArch Atari800 core, and Altirra path settings? [Completeness, Plan §Summary]
- [x] CHK003 Are requirements defined for preserving existing browse, search, scroll, detail, extras, version-selection, favorites, and back-navigation workflows across platforms? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK004 Are platform-scoped settings requirements complete for media folders, emulator profiles, preferred emulator, import state, active platform, and last-used platform? [Completeness, Data Model §Platform Folder Settings]
- [x] CHK005 Are requirements defined for C64 compatibility preservation while adding Atari 800 platform behavior? [Completeness, Import/Launch Contract §C64 Compatibility Contract]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is "equivalent user-selected Atari 800 GameBase MDB" defined clearly enough to distinguish acceptable Atari 800 sources from unsupported MDBs? [Clarity, Import/Launch Contract §Atari 800 Import Contract]
- [x] CHK007 Are the Atari 800 folder names Games, Music, Photos, and Screenshots defined with expected content and whether each is required or optional? [Clarity, Data Model §Platform Folder Settings]
- [x] CHK008 Is "functionally consistent" for cross-platform workflows defined with enough concrete workflow examples to avoid subjective interpretation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK009 Are RetroArch Atari800 and Altirra emulator profiles distinguished clearly by required settings, launch inputs, and failure behavior? [Clarity, Import/Launch Contract §RetroArch Atari 800 Launch Contract]
- [x] CHK010 Are platform capability terms such as `sid`, `sap`, `generic`, `none`, `inAppEmulation`, and `launchExtensions` defined clearly enough for UI and launch decisions? [Clarity, Data Model §Platform]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Are Atari 800 required folder settings consistent between the plan, data model, quickstart, and import/launch contract? [Consistency, Plan §Summary]
- [x] CHK012 Are platform-scoped query requirements consistent between spec requirements, Tauri IPC contract, and data model uniqueness rules? [Consistency, Spec §FR-008]
- [x] CHK013 Are music capability requirements consistent between the spec's SID limitation, data model media capability values, and UI capability contract? [Consistency, Spec §FR-017]
- [x] CHK014 Are default emulator requirements consistent between "RetroArch for every platform" and "Altirra as Atari 800-specific external emulator"? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012]
- [x] CHK015 Are import failure and launch failure requirements consistently required to identify the affected platform across spec and contracts? [Consistency, Spec §FR-018]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK016 Are success criteria measurable for platform switching, startup restoration, platform-scoped search/favorites, emulator configuration, and workflow parity? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [x] CHK017 Is SC-008 specific enough to determine which initial supported platforms and workflows must be covered before implementation is accepted? [Measurability, Spec §SC-008]
- [x] CHK018 Are acceptance scenarios for User Story 3 sufficient to validate both RetroArch Atari800 and Altirra requirements, or is Altirra underrepresented in the user-facing spec? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3]
- [x] CHK019 Are acceptance criteria defined for unimported Atari 800 selection routing to the Atari 800 import flow rather than a generic platform import? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §User Story 2]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK020 Are primary, alternate, and recovery requirements documented for selecting an unimported platform, canceling import, retrying failed import, and returning to an imported platform? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK021 Are requirements specified for overlapping game titles across platforms, including search, favorites, and detail navigation behavior? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 5]
- [x] CHK022 Are requirements defined for switching platforms while preserving or resetting scroll position, selected game, and BigBox focus state per platform? [Gap, Plan §Project Structure]
- [x] CHK023 Are requirements documented for platforms with no music support, platforms with generic music support, and C64 SID support without conflicting UI behavior? [Coverage, Data Model §Media Capability]
- [x] CHK024 Are requirements defined for representative multi-file Atari 800 launch cases, including whether playlist behavior differs between RetroArch and Altirra? [Gap, Import/Launch Contract §RetroArch Atari 800 Launch Contract]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK025 Are requirements defined for invalid, missing, or wrong-platform MDB files during Atari 800 import? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK026 Are requirements defined for missing RetroArch executable, missing Atari800 core, missing Altirra executable, and stale saved emulator paths? [Edge Case, Import/Launch Contract §RetroArch Atari 800 Launch Contract]
- [x] CHK027 Are requirements specified for missing optional media folders versus missing required Atari 800 folder settings without contradicting import behavior? [Edge Case, Import/Launch Contract §Atari 800 Import Contract]
- [x] CHK028 Are requirements defined for generated launch artifact cleanup and source-file protection after failed or canceled launches? [Edge Case, Data Model §Launch Artifact]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK029 Are performance requirements for platform switching and large-library browsing quantified for both C64 and Atari 800 contexts? [Non-Functional, Plan §Technical Context]
- [x] CHK030 Are local-first privacy and path ownership requirements documented for platform-scoped MDB paths, media folders, and emulator paths? [Non-Functional, Constitution §Local-First Library Ownership]
- [x] CHK031 Are accessibility and gamepad/keyboard navigation requirements specified for the platform switcher and Atari 800 import/settings flows? [Gap, Constitution §Fast Browsing and Controller-Grade UX]
- [x] CHK032 Are migration requirements specified for existing flat C64 settings into platform-scoped settings without losing user paths? [Gap, Plan §Technical Context]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK033 Are assumptions about Atari 800 GameBase folder structure validated or flagged as assumptions needing implementation research? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK034 Are dependencies on RetroArch Atari800 core and Altirra availability documented with platform support boundaries? [Dependency, Plan §Technical Context]
- [x] CHK035 Are assumptions about using one platform-scoped SQLite database versus separate platform databases documented strongly enough for task planning? [Assumption, Research §Platform-Aware Import Pipeline]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK036 Is there any conflict between "Music is required for Atari 800 settings" and "missing optional media folders do not block import"? [Conflict, Data Model §Platform Folder Settings]
- [x] CHK037 Is "Photos" distinguished from screenshots, box art, musician photos, or scraped media clearly enough for settings and media resolution requirements? [Ambiguity, Plan §Summary]
- [x] CHK038 Is Altirra launch support required to fully launch games in the first implementation slice, or is a dry-run launch test acceptable for initial acceptance? [Ambiguity, Import/Launch Contract §Altirra Launch Contract]

## Notes

- Focus areas: platform workflow parity, Atari 800 import/settings, RetroArch Atari800, Altirra, platform-scoped data.
- Depth: Standard.
- Audience/timing: PR reviewer before `/speckit-tasks` and implementation.
- Completed: 2026-06-27 after user decisions for CHK001-CHK038 were incorporated into spec, plan, data model, contracts, research, and quickstart.
